incorrects.description: Student selected D (park bench with varying people): believes varying groups still count as equal groups

steps.text: Look at the benches closely. Are there the same number of people on each bench? For equal groups, every group needs the same amount.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Scenarios A, B, and C highlight together to show they all contain equal groups.

steps.text: Let me show you how to spot equal groups. The egg carton has 2 rows of 6 eggs. The parking lot has 3 rows of 4 cars. The bookshelf has 5 shelves of 3 books. Each one has groups with the same amount in every group. That's what makes them equal groups. The park benches have different numbers of people, so that's not equal groups.
